title: 1098 and 1099 forms
description: Practice your understanding of a109 and 1099 tax forms

assessment_items.question_content:
Ivory just completed the first year of culinary school. Her grandparents have been paying her tuition fees. 

**What 1098 form should they expect to receive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Clara has been paying interest on her loan for art school. 

**What 1098 tax form should she expect to get from her lender?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

Peter and Clara's parents donated their car to charity. 

**What 1098 tax form should they look out for?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

Alejandro is a [[☃ definition 1]] software developer who has been working independently, developing software for various companies.

**What 1099 form should he expect to receive from each company?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: when you work for yourself rather than a company, often doing different jobs for multiple clients

assessment_items.question_content:
Isabella's savings account earned some interest this year. 

**What kind of 1099 form is she likely to receive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Rafael has been unemployed and has been receiving unemployment benefits.

**Which 1099 form should he look out for?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You have a salaried job where you earn $\$35{,}000$ a year. You also receive a 1098 form that shows you've paid $\$5{,}000$ in mortgage interest.

**If you choose to itemize your deductions, what will be your *taxable income*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your job pays you $\$50{,}000$ per year. You get a 1098-E form that shows $\$2{,}500$ in student loan interest.

**If you choose to itemize your deductions, what will your *taxable income* be?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You earn $\$55{,}000$ a year from your job. You've received a 1098-T form showing you've paid $\$10{,}000$ in tuition and related expenses.

**If you itemize your deductions, what will your *taxable income* be?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You have a part-time job that pays you $\$20{,}000$ a year. In addition to your part time work, you also worked as a [[☃ definition 1]] designer. You received a 1099-MISC form that reports $\$5{,}000$.

**Considering both your job and freelance work, how much of your earnings is considered *taxable income*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: when you work for yourself rather than a company, often doing different jobs for multiple clients

assessment_items.question_content:
As a hobby you love making guitars and have managed to turn this talent into extra income. Last year this hobby  generated $\$8{,}000$ and it resulted in you receiving a 1099-MISC form from the guitar store. Your full-time job pays you $\$50{,}000$ a year. 

**What is your *taxable income*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

As a skilled mechanic you make $\$95{,}000$ a year. You also  [[☃ definition 1]] gauge clusters for a small shop which earned you $\$4{,}500$. You received a 1099-MISC form from the shop.

**What is your *taxable income*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: make like new and/or restore to newer working condition
